_comment: Widget spec for the glyph-wheel color picker. Loaded once at startup via `include_str!` so WASM can read it. First step toward user-authored widgets — everything that defines the picker's *appearance* (glyphs, proportions, copy) lives here; the pure-function layout math stays in Rust because it depends on measured shaped-glyph advances and screen dimensions that JSON can't express.

geometry._comment: Size constants shared by the layout and render paths. The picker is sized as a *widget* — its base font size is derived from the screen's shorter side (`target_frac * short_axis * size_scale / wheel_side_in_fonts`), then clamped to [`font_min`, `font_max`]. `size_scale` defaults to 1.0 and is mutated by the right-mouse-button drag-to-resize gesture; it lives on the picker state and resets each open. `outline_px` sets the glyph-outline thickness at the `font_max` baseline (the stamp count is canonical in baumhard's `OutlineStyle::offsets`). `transparent_backdrop` skips the opaque rect underneath so canvas content shows through.

hint_text_contextual: Esc cancel  ·  ࿕ commit  ·  drag to move  ·  RMB resize
hint_text_standalone: ࿕ commit  ·  drag to move  ·  RMB resize
_mutator_spec_comment: Declarative mutator-tree shape for the picker overlay. Root is a Void on channel 0; each Repeat expands to N consecutive Singles on channels [channel_base, channel_base+count), skipping declared skip_indices. Every Single's AreaDelta carries the same 8-field template; bare CellField names pull per-cell values from the section-keyed area lookup at apply time, Operation::Assign is baked in for every cell. See src/application/mutator_builder/ for the walker.
